game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters

danger_signals.visual_form: Cyber Shredder as tournament contestant and final boss
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; tournament serves as cover for confrontation with nemesis

danger_signals.visual_form: Story mode objective: rescue April O'Neil and Splinter from Karai's clutches
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to allied characters; motivates Turtle participation in tournament

danger_signals.visual_form: War (monstrous purple creature with claws), Armaggon (mutant shark), Chrome Dome (android), Wingnut (bat-like alien)
danger_signals.meaning: Tournament draws dangerous entities; visual otherness signals threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Palette-swapped duplicate versions of player Turtles available as opponents
danger_signals.meaning: Corrupted or controlled versions represent internal/identity threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Mobile headquarters with logo display; day-time background (JP version shows logo clearly)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe base of operations; Turtles travel across US in secure transport during story mode

safety_signals.visual_form: Rescue objectives; Splinter's face appears on flying monitor power-up dispensers
safety_signals.meaning: Master/mentor and ally presence signals faction protection; Splinter's direct involvement in mechanics

safety_signals.visual_form: Only four Turtles are playable in story mode; other characters relegated to opponent status
safety_signals.meaning: Player-controlled faction (TMNT) vs. outsiders; unity in purpose

faction_indicators.color_identity: Individual character colors: Leonardo (blue), Raphael (red), Michaelangelo (orange), Donatello (purple)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Turtle Blimp logo; Splinter connection as master/sensei
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Clone Turtles appear as palette-swapped opponents; Cyber Shredder represents corrupted technology vs. organic mutants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament participation forced; story mode shows cross-US travel via Turtle Blimp

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shredder as tournament organizer/contestant; Karai as operative in story
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Chrome Dome (android servant previously created by Shredder); technological vs. biological distinction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament location; kidnapping operation targeting April/Splinter

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Aska (ninja girl seeking dojo establishment); War (Four Horsemen creature); Armaggon (mutant); Wingnut (alien)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visual distinction through monster design and non-ninja aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament draws from diverse backgrounds; motivations independent of Turtle/Shredder conflict

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple fighters gathered; tournament bracket system; stage variety across US locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters participate in combat matches; Turtles travel via Blimp collecting information

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: April O'Neil and Splinter held captive (story mode context)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Turtles must defeat tournament opponents to progress toward rescue; forced participation rather than choice

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Turtle Blimp travels; visual transition from stage to stage across US geography
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sequential combat encounters; information gathering between fights; final confrontation with Shredder

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: JP version: Turtle Blimp logo clearly displayed, day-time stage background; US version: obscured/absent visual elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: JP version: additional voice clips from Turtles in Time; character palette selection via Start button; rebalanced move damage

environmental_storytelling.example: Turtle Blimp travel across US in story mode communicates expanding scope of threat; journey toward confrontation
Mix of mutants, aliens, androids, and independent humans in tournament indicates post-apocalyptic/mutant-integrated society
Splinter's face on flying monitor that drops power balls communicates master's guidance during combat; direct mentorship mechanic
Clone Turtle opponents suggest corruption or duplication; visual identity loss as threat
JP version's clear Turtle Blimp logo and day-time backgrounds vs. US obscured version communicates different marketing/censorship approach but same world state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Appears as contestant but functions as nemesis; mechanical/technological nature suggests threat escalation
Identical movesets to originals but color-differentiated; flicker effect on NES indicates strain/instability of duplication
Described as alien in Tournament mode, mutant in story mode; inconsistent categorization suggests ambiguous threat status
Android created by Shredder; presence in tournament suggests escaped or repurposed servant; technology faction representative
Travel via Blimp collecting information; sequential combat victories; move toward rescue mission; unified faction behavior
Appears as power-up source (face on monitor); presence in captive state motivates player action; mentor role communicated through mechanic

notes: SNES version emphasizes tournament as narrative frame for Karai threat and rescue mission. Story mode structure (travel via Blimp, information gathering, sequential opponents) communicates expanding world scope. Faction distinction relies heavily on visual character design (mutant vs. alien vs. android vs. ninja) rather than explicit environmental markers. Regional differences (JP vs. US/EU) primarily affect visual polish and audio rather than world-state communication, suggesting consistent narrative core across versions. Power-up mechanic integrates Splinter visually into combat, reinforcing mentor faction presence. Cyber Shredder designation emphasizes technological threat layer. Clone Turtle opponents provide identity/corruption storytelling.
